description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun with a robust black metal receiver and barrel, complemented by polished wooden furniture including the stock, foregrip, and pistol grip. The weapon features a prominent round drum magazine mounted beneath the receiver, characteristic of early 20th-century designs. The barrel is long and cylindrical, with a simple front sight post and a rear sight integrated into the receiver. The wooden stock is fixed and ergonomically shaped, providing a stable shoulder rest. The overall construction suggests a blend of steel and wood, with visible screws and mechanical details on the receiver and magazine.
description: A vintage-style submachine gun with a black metal body, wooden stock and grips, and a large drum magazine beneath the receiver.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the gun, highlighting the wooden stock, foregrip, and the large drum magazine.
description_view_1: Front view emphasizing the circular drum magazine and the alignment of the barrel and wooden grips.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the features of view 0, with clear visibility of the wooden components and drum magazine.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden stock and the back of the receiver, with the drum magazine visible below.
attribute_stock_design: fixed wooden stock with ergonomic contour
attribute_iron_sight_configuration: simple notch rear sight with fixed front post
